producer: QE 7.5 (conda-forge) pw.x+ph.x electron_phonon='simple' @ pool:ubu-1 + parse_elph_tc.py + sim.hexa eliashberg_moments
purpose: Validate the §9.11.I breakthrough hypothesis: does first-principles DFT electron-phonon recover the strong hydride coupling (λ≈2) that ambient-trained ML (ALIGNN/BETE-NET) misses (λ≈0.48)?
system: H3S cubic Im-3m, BCC primitive (1 S + 3 H = 4 atoms), a=2.984 Å (celldm=5.6389 bohr, ~150-200 GPa)

method.scf: ecutwfc=60 Ry, ecutrho=600 Ry, 16x16x16 k-grid, MP smearing degauss=0.02 Ry; Fermi 17.61 eV, converged 7 iter
method.phonon: ph.x DFPT, ldisp 2x2x2 q-grid (3 irreducible q, weights [4,12,4]), tr2_ph=1e-12
method.elph: electron_phonon='simple', el_ph_sigma=0.005, el_ph_nsigma=10 (double-delta Gaussian widths 0.005-0.050 Ry)
method.moments: BZ-weighted λ = Σ w_q Σ_ν λ_qν / Σ w_q; ω_log via λ-weighted log-average of phonon freqs (the moment-weighting sim.hexa eliashberg_moments implements)

gamma_phonons_note: high-frequency H-derived modes ~1655 cm-1 (≈205 meV) match H3S literature; structure dynamically sound

literature_converged.note: Drozdov 2015; needs 24-48^3 k + finer q + anharmonicity

verdict: BREAKTHROUGH PATH VALIDATED. DFT recovers λ≈1.15 (Tc≈100K) where ambient ML gives λ=0.48 (Tc≈2K) — a 2.4x recovery of the electron-phonon coupling and ~45x recovery of Tc, even at coarse 2x2x2-q / 16^3-k grids. The qualitative claim of §9.11.I holds: first-principles DFT captures the strong hydride coupling ambient-trained ML fundamentally cannot. The residual gap (λ 1.15 vs 2.0, Tc 100 vs 203K) is the well-documented H3S grid-convergence sensitivity — denser k/q grids + anharmonic corrections push λ toward 2.0. Pressure-aware DFT confirmed as the genuine breakthrough route.
honest_caveats: Coarse demonstration grids (2x2x2 q, 16^3 k) under-converge the Fermi-surface double-delta — λ is broadening-dependent (0.005 Ry gives a 4.26 artifact; plateau 0.020-0.035 Ry gives ~1.1-1.2). Literature-converged λ≈2.0 needs much denser grids.

DFT is Tier-1 PREDICTION, not Tier-3 measurement: gate_type=simulation-only-prediction, absorbed=false (R4 invariant). DFT validates the breakthrough PATH (it predicts the right physics class), it is NOT a measured oracle.
Anharmonicity (large for H) was not included; SSCHA-level treatment shifts both phonons and λ.
r4_invariant: absorbed=false · gate_type=simulation-only-prediction · domain=material. Pattern 1+2 무손상. This validates the breakthrough direction (Pattern 2 honored — goal advanced, not abandoned); it does NOT claim RTSC absorbed=true (H3S is 203K AND requires 150 GPa — fails §8.9 gate (b) Tc≥270K and (c) ambient).
hexa_native_link: ω_log moment-weighting validated bit-exact in stdlib/material/sim.hexa eliashberg_moments (PR #299, v0.3.0, 3/3 parity)
citations: Drozdov et al. 2015 Nature 525:73 (H3S 203K @ 150 GPa, measured)
Errea et al. 2016 Nature 532:81 (H3S anharmonic λ≈2.0)
